description: Multi-phase settlement with preview support. Antecedent is the immediately preceding message in the settlement chain. Phases: preview-request (buyer requests content preview after match); preview (exchange delivers randomized preview chunks to buyer); buyer-accept/buyer-reject (sent by buyer — after preview for content >= 500 tokens, after match for content < 500 tokens); put-accept/put-reject (sent by exchange after receiving put); deliver (exchange sends full content to buyer); complete (buyer confirms receipt and hash); dispute (buyer contests content — legacy, see small-content-dispute); small-content-dispute (buyer disputes content < 500 tokens — auto-refund, no operator verdict, -3 reputation per refund). Sender roles: buyer sends preview-request, buyer-accept, buyer-reject, complete, dispute, small-content-dispute; exchange operator sends put-accept, put-reject, preview, deliver.
provenance_level_note: Buyer phases (preview-request, buyer-accept, buyer-reject, complete, dispute, small-content-dispute) require claimed. Operator phases (put-accept, put-reject, preview, deliver) require present. See pkg/exchange/provenance.go settlePhaseRequiredLevel.

args.name: target
args.description: Message ID of the preceding message in the settlement chain. For preview-request: the exchange:match message. For preview: the settle(preview-request) message. For buyer-accept/buyer-reject: the settle(preview) message (>= 500 tokens) or the exchange:match message (< 500 tokens). For deliver: the settle(buyer-accept) message. For complete/dispute/small-content-dispute: the settle(deliver) message.

args.name: phase
args.description: Settlement phase. Composed into exchange:phase:<value> tag.

args.name: accepted
args.description: Buyer's accept/reject decision. Used in buyer-accept and buyer-reject phases.

args.name: reason
args.description: Reason for rejection or dispute. Optional.

args.name: price
args.description: Agreed price in scrip (micro-tokens). Required in put-accept and complete phases. In preview phase, this is the purchase_price (price for unseen content after preview discount).

args.name: content_hash_verified
args.description: Whether content hash was verified against delivered content. Required in complete phase.

args.name: dispute_type
args.description: Type of dispute. Required in dispute phase. Not used in small-content-dispute (which is unconditional).

args.name: verdict
args.description: Settlement verdict. Composed into exchange:verdict:<value> tag. Omitted for intermediate phases (deliver, put-accept, put-reject, preview-request, preview). auto-refunded is used for small-content-dispute.

args.name: preview_chunks
args.description: Array of preview chunk objects, each with {content: string, position: integer, length: integer}. Required in preview phase. The exchange assembles 5 non-overlapping random chunks totaling 15-25% of the entry content. Chunks respect content-type boundaries (function-level for code, paragraph-level for prose, record-level for data).

args.name: preview_chunk_count
args.description: Number of preview chunks delivered. Required in preview phase. Typically 5.

args.name: content_type
args.description: Content type used for chunking strategy in preview phase. Determines chunk boundary rules: code uses function/block boundaries, prose uses paragraph boundaries, data uses record boundaries. Required in preview phase.

args.name: base_price
args.description: Full price of the entry before preview discount. Included in preview phase so the buyer can infer the preview fraction: preview_fraction = 1 - (price / base_price).

args.name: content_hash
args.description: SHA-256 hash of the full content. Included in preview phase for later verification at complete time.

args.name: auto_refund
args.description: Whether this dispute triggers an automatic full refund. Always true for small-content-dispute phase. No operator verdict required.
